summary:
The oral mucosa is predominantly made up of stratified squamous epithelium, with variation in keratinization depending on location—for example, non-keratinized in linings and keratinized in hard palates and gums. This consistent underlying tissue type makes "stratified squamous" the best overall descriptor for all regions of the oral cavity.  

Final Answer: \boxed{B}

Second sentence should explain why one option over others—that 'stratified squamous' encompasses both types due to shared foundational architecture—even though some parts are specifically keratinized.
